item.title: Thesis report Week#6
item.description: Updates: Multisensor tracking - summary When measurements are obtained from multiple sensors, there is a need to distinguish between different cases: Asynchronous measurements - i.e the sampling times are different for each sensor and not necessarily fixed. In this project's context, both the camera and the radar sensors, even though the sensors can process the data at a fixed time interval, an actual target measurement might arrive at arbitrary time samples due to environmental uncertainty.

item.title: Autonomous navigation: 31388
item.description: Autonomous navigation in a structured environment.

item.title: Thesis report Week#4-5
item.description: Updates: This week I have been working on Work on filtering visualization Implement multiple moving objects in the Simulation Implement multiple tracked objects in the simulation Implement Particle Filter Algorithm for single target Implement EKF with Probabilistic data Assosiation (PDAF) Implement PF with Probabilistic Data Assosiation (PDAF) [Multitarget-multisensor Tracking: Principles and Techniques : Yakkov Bar-Shalom Xiao-Rong Li] Filtering visualization Filters visualization for single target.

item.title: Thesis report Week#3
item.description: The essence of estimation The generation of information that has the following properties quality (accuracy/reliability) higher than the raw measurements contains information not directly available in the measurements [Multitarget-multisensor Tracking: Principles and Techniques : Yakkov Bar-Shalom Xiao-Rong Li] Mathematical view of state estimation. No access to variables inside the first two blocks The only accessible variable are the measurements which are affected by noise.
